message:
fix: use [a-zA-Z0-9] instead of \w in tokenizer regex to exclude underscores

The RegexTokenizer pattern \w+(?:-\w+)* includes underscores as word
characters, but cass_sanitize_query and cass_generate_edge_ngrams both
treat underscores as word boundaries (via char::is_alphanumeric which
excludes _). This mismatch meant:

- Indexing: "hello_world" → one token "hello_world"
- Query: "hello_world" → sanitized to "hello world" → no match

Changed the regex to [a-zA-Z0-9]+(?:-[a-zA-Z0-9]+)* which explicitly
excludes underscores, matching the behaviour of the original
SimpleTokenizer and the rest of the cass pipeline.

Added test verifying underscore-splitting consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
